title: n8n shutting down due to ECS health check failures
symptom: n8n logs show repeated 'Database connection timed out' messages followed by 'Received SIGTERM. Shutting down...'. This indicates that ECS is marking the task as unhealthy and restarting it.
not_this_if: No database connection issues are present
root_cause: The ECS health check is likely configured to monitor a database-dependent endpoint. If the database experiences brief hiccups, the health check fails, causing ECS to restart the n8n container. This creates a cycle of restarts during temporary database unavailability.
fix_steps: Review the ALB/ECS health check path and thresholds.
Change the health check to a basic 'service is up' endpoint or relax the timeout/unhealthy threshold.
Add a grace period to the health check settings.
Tune the n8n Postgres connection/pool timeout environment variables.
agent_instruction: Adjust the ECS health check settings to prevent unnecessary restarts of the n8n container.
